analyst_briefing: US dominates signals with SEC FY2025 enforcement ($17.9B penalties) exposing D1-D5 gaps: biopharma BO opacity (HIGH, architecture), NCUA deregulation (ELEVATED, structural enabler risk), HRSA program vulnerabilities. Mixed Tier A signal—high enforcement coexists with persistent enablement, per architecture-over-incident. Kenya emerges as D3/D4 concern: TBML-mobile Al-Shabaab channels (ELEVATED, capacity-driven). Cayman funds perpetuate D2 opacity. Standing trackers stable except T4 adapting on BO gaps. Severity upgrades NCUA to ELEVATED for framework shift. Cross-flags: SCEM/FCW on Kenya CTF, WDM on US BO. Tooling ready: US stable, Kenya increasing; inventory prioritizes mixers/scams/TBML. No critical systemic shifts; enforcement episodes do not alter enabler architectures.
